author: @twestgard2
text:
I work around people who try all sorts of clever things with table saws, ladders, electrical devices, and heavy objects. I no longer work around the LONG list of people who I’ve seen brutally injured because they “just gave it a go.” There is something worse than not trying something, and that’s getting injured doing something dangerous. The top ten most commonly injured workers in the US are all in the logging, construction, and agricultural trades (plus air freight pilots, disturbingly enough).

Yes, maybe white collar people need more encouragement to “just give it a go.” Blue collar people are giving a lot of things a go and they have the broken backs and missing limbs and eyes to prove it. Safety is important and it merits a mention.

replies.author: @wongowonga
replies.text:
I get your point, however most of Robs ideas are not dangerous if you have common sense. There have been some that I wouldn't feel comfortable doing with my knowledge and skill set. If that's the case, I don't do it!   ....Yet! I just keep digging until I get somewhere!?

Surely there has to be another channel that teaches common sense... or is there? ;)

replies.author: @twestgard2
replies.text: @wongowonga yeah, it’s all about good judgment. Too much fear holds you back, not enough gets you killed.

replies.author: @wongowonga
replies.text: @twestgard2 Very true. I guess it's a bit like crossing the road. At first we need help and guidance, eventually we learn how to do it without holding hands. There could always be that moment you take your eye off the ball and get caught out but we still cross the road.

replies.author: @wongowonga
replies.text: @twestgard2 BTW, your words have resonated with me, very well said and does apply to much of life.

replies.author: @neontv6605
replies.text: @MyNameIsLukey I would do it the same way Luke sure it was 1400 pound of bitz. I was scared the first time. I understand it better now. I would not be scared to do it this time but still use the same methods to complete it.Clean up before starting an ground yourself then if it don't fit do not force it at all. Slowly work it an the cpu needs thermal paste we need to be careful here as it needs not loads but enough to cover it perfect but not on the sides just on top look at this online for thicknesses.
